channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Eight Hundred Kilometers
item.description: By Kira Yuki Tanaka, Colony Correspondent & Culture Writer -- The Kadmiel Chronicle I was running the Ner River path before sunrise — which, if you've been following this column long enough, you...

item.title: How I Built My Personal Site as a Digital Identity Hub (And How You Can Too)
item.description: I never imagined a simple, free personal site could be this powerful. My goal wasn't just to get a URL—I wanted to build a "command center" for my entire digital identity. In this guide, I'll walk...

item.title: Why I Needed a Warning Light for AI Spend
item.description: A few weeks ago I caught myself doing the thing I hate most as a solo dev: pretending a problem was "under control" because I had a rough idea of the numbers. I was shipping fast, using Claude for...

item.title: I Built a Production-Ready Next.js SaaS Starter Kit in 2 Weekends — Here's Everything I Included
item.description: Every time I started a new SaaS project, I found myself spending the first week doing the exact same thing — setting up authentication, connecting a database, wiring up payments, building a...

item.title: I Bought `anshu.uk` — Here’s Why Every Developer Should Own Their Name
item.description: Most developers treat portfolios as a side project. I decided to treat mine like infrastructure. 👉 Live: https://www.anshu.uk/ 🧠 The Problem: You Don’t Exist on Google Search “Anshu” and you’ll...

item.title: The Factory Must Grow: I Replaced Myself with AI. Now What?
item.description: tl;dr -- I made an orchestration system that creates PRDs, writes code, opens PRs and handles review feedback. And then I realised I'd automated myself out of the parts I once called my job. The...

item.title: I Tried to Distill Myself into Training Data. Then Something Got Complicated.
item.description: A few days ago, I did something a little strange: I wrote a script that scanned through months of my own conversation logs, behavioral records, and thinking traces, then extracted 540 training...

item.title: Smart Contract Escrow: How Trustless Betting Works
item.description: Introduction One of the most exciting applications of blockchain technology is enabling trustless interactions between parties who don't know or necessarily trust each other. In blockchain gaming...

item.title: Freshping Migration Guide: 6 Steps Before June 4, 2026
item.description: If you are reading this, you already know Freshping is gone. The service was discontinued on March 6, 2026, and Freshworks gave everyone a 90-day window before all accounts, monitors, and history...

item.title: Over-Documentation Is Not Waste for AI Systems
item.description: What looks redundant to humans is often exactly what makes knowledge usable for AI. That is one of the biggest mindset shifts teams run into when they start designing documentation for AI-assisted...

item.title: The perfect successor to Lost has been hiding from me for years
item.description: Ever since Jack decided to stay behind in 2010, I've been searching for something to give me the same feeling that Lost did. I crave a big mystery with a huge cast and more secrets than I can...

item.title: Walmart is updating its 4K streaming box with Gemini and Matter support
item.description: It hasn't been officially announced, but Walmart's website now has a listing (you may need to set your location to Ventura, California) for an updated version of its budget-friendly Onn 4K Pro...

item.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
item.description: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

item.title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
item.description: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

item.title: Gitar, a startup that uses agents to secure code, emerges from stealth with $9 million
item.description: The company uses AI to review code that, more often than not these days, has also been generated by AI.

item.title: This startup is betting tokenmaxxing will create the next compute giant
item.description: Parasail raised $32 million in a Series A, signaling a fractured future of models and compute.

item.title: More phone cameras should come with telephoto lenses
item.description: Upgrading your phone with a camera grip attachment is one thing. But doll-sized telephoto lenses that you mount on top of the rear camera? C'mon. I wrote the Vivo X300 off as a gimmick, a funny...

item.title: Adobe embraces conversational AI editing, marking a ‘fundamental shift’ in creative work
item.description: Adobe is fully embracing AI tools that enable creators to edit their work using descriptive prompts, instead of manually using specific Creative Cloud apps. The software giant's new Firefly AI...

item.title: Amazon’s new Fire TV Stick HD is its ‘slimmest ever’
item.description: Amazon has taken the wraps off a new Fire TV Stick HD that it says is its slimmest yet. At $34.99, the device offers a design that Amazon says is 30 percent thinner than the previous generation,...

item.title: Actively Exploited nginx-ui Flaw (CVE-2026-33032) Enables Full Nginx Server Takeover
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting nginx-ui, an open-source, web-based Nginx management tool, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is...

item.title: Nothing makes it easy to share files between any Android phone and a Mac
item.description: I test Android phones for a living, but I write about them using a company-supplied MacBook Air. Both platforms are great in their own right, but they're not so great at talking to one another. On...

item.title: April Patch Tuesday Fixes Critical Flaws Across SAP, Adobe, Microsoft, Fortinet, and More
item.description: A number of critical vulnerabilities impacting products from Adobe, Fortinet, Microsoft, and SAP have taken center stage in April's Patch Tuesday releases. Topping the list is an SQL injection...

item.title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. NASA is building the first nuclear reactor-powered...

item.title: The new Tomodachi Life is made to be shared — even if Nintendo doesn’t want you to
item.description: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is hard to explain. The best way to understand is to see it in action; a screenshot of Handsome Squidward and Bob Belcher falling in love over their shared...

item.title: Microsoft faces fresh Windows Recall security concerns
item.description: When Microsoft tried to launch Recall, an AI-powered Windows feature that screenshots most of what you do on your PC, it was labeled a "disaster" for cybersecurity and a "privacy nightmare." After...

item.title: Deterministic + Agentic AI: The Architecture Exposure Validation Requires
item.description: Few technologies have moved from experimentation to boardroom mandate as quickly as AI. Across industries, leadership teams have embraced its broader potential, and boards, investors, and...

item.title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
item.description: From inside a money-laundering center in Cambodia, an employee opens a popular Vietnamese banking app on his phone. The app asks him to upload a photo associated with the account, so he clicks on...

item.title: Snap is laying off 16 percent of its staff as it leans into AI
item.description: Snap is laying off roughly 16 percent of its global workforce in a cost-cutting effort to chase improved profitability with the help of AI. The cuts will impact around 1,000 full-time employees,...

item.title: AI Slop Is Making the Internet Fake-Happy
item.description: A new study examines the impact of the rise of AI-generated websites on the internet—and found some surprising results.

item.title: Spotify now sells printed books
item.description: Spotify is rolling out some new updates for book lovers, expanding audiobook listening features and allowing users to buy physical publications through the platform. Readers in the US and UK can...
